Lagundi.name: Lagundi
Lagundi.description: Lagundi is a shrub that grows up to 5 meters tall. It has distinctive five-fingered leaves (palmate compound leaves) and small blue to lavender flowers. It is one of the most widely used medicinal plants in the Philippines, recognized by the Department of Health for its effectiveness.
preparation_steps: Boil a handful of fresh or dried leaves in 2 cups of water for 15 minutes
Let it cool and strain the liquid
Drink as tea, 1 cup 3 times daily
For cough relief, add honey to taste
Lagundi.cultural_relevance: Lagundi has been used in Philippine traditional medicine for centuries. It is one of the ten herbal medicines endorsed by the Department of Health (DOH) for its proven medicinal properties. The plant is commonly found in backyards and is often the first remedy for cough and colds in Filipino households.
Lagundi.detailed_description: Lagundi (Vitex negundo) is one of ten medicinal plants officially endorsed by the Philippine Department of Health (DOH) for its proven effectiveness in treating respiratory ailments including cough, asthma, and pharyngitis.

waray.description: An Lagundi in usa nga shrub nga nagtubo tubtob 5 metros kahitaas. Mayda ini nga talagsa nga lima nga mga bulong (palmate compound leaves) ngan mga gagmay nga azul ngan lavender nga mga burak. Ini in usa ha mga gigamit nga medicinal nga tanom ha Pilipinas, ginkilala han Department of Health para ha iya pagka-epektibo.
medicinal_uses: Ginpapahimuot an sakit han ulo ngan rheumatism
preparation_steps: Kalad-an an usa nga damo hin fresh o dried nga mga bulong ha 2 nga baso hin tubig para ha 15 minutos
Hayaan ini nga malip-ot ngan ibulag an likido
Inumon komo tsaa, 1 nga baso 3 nga beses ha adlaw
Para ha pagpahimuot han ubo, dugdugan hin honey para ha taste
waray.cultural_relevance: An Lagundi in gingagamit ha Philippine traditional nga tambal para ha mga siglo. Ini in usa ha mga napulo nga herbal nga tambal nga gin-endorso han Department of Health (DOH) para ha iya ginprobar nga medicinal nga mga kabtang. An tanom in komun nga mahiagi ha backyards ngan agsob in syahan nga tambal para ha ubo ngan sip-on ha Filipino nga mga household.
waray.detailed_description: An Lagundi (Vitex negundo) in usa ha mga napulo nga medicinal nga tanom nga opisyal nga gin-endorso han Philippine Department of Health (DOH) para ha iya ginprobar nga pagka-epektibo ha pagtambal han respiratory nga mga sakit upod an ubo, asthma, ngan pharyngitis.

Sambong.name: Sambong
Sambong.description: Sambong is a coarse erect annual herb that grows up to 1.5 meters tall. It has soft, hairy leaves that emit a camphor-like scent when crushed. The plant produces yellow flowers in clusters at the top. It thrives in open grasslands and waste places throughout the Philippines.
preparation_steps: Boil 10-15 leaves in 3 cups of water for 20 minutes
Drink 1 cup, 3 times daily after meals
Sambong.cultural_relevance: Sambong is officially recognized by the Philippine Department of Health as a medicinal plant for its diuretic properties and effectiveness in treating kidney stones. It has been used by traditional healers (albularyo) for generations and remains a popular herbal remedy in rural communities.
Sambong.detailed_description: Sambong (Blumea balsamifera) is officially recognized by Philippine DOH for its diuretic properties and effectiveness in dissolving kidney stones and treating urinary tract infections.

waray.description: An Sambong in usa nga coarse erect annual herb nga nagtubo tubtob 1.5 metros kahitaas. Mayda ini nga maupay, hairy nga mga bulong nga nagpapakita hin camphor-like nga scent pagkatapos gindurog. An tanom nagprodyus hin mga pula nga burak ha mga clusters ha ibabaw. Nagtubo ini ha open grasslands ngan waste places ha bug-os nga Pilipinas.
preparation_steps: Hugasan an fresh nga mga bulong nga maupay
Kalad-an an 10-15 nga mga bulong ha 3 nga baso hin tubig para ha 20 minutos
Inumon 1 nga baso, 3 nga beses ha adlaw pagkatapos han pagkaon
waray.cultural_relevance: An Sambong in opisyal nga ginkilala han Philippine Department of Health komo usa nga medicinal nga tanom para ha iya diuretic nga mga kabtang ngan pagka-epektibo ha pagtambal han kidney stones. Ini in gingagamit han mga traditional healers (albularyo) para ha mga henerasyon ngan nagpapabilin komo usa nga popular nga herbal remedy ha rural nga mga komunidad.
waray.detailed_description: An Sambong (Blumea balsamifera) in opisyal nga ginkilala han Philippine DOH para ha iya diuretic nga mga kabtang ngan pagka-epektibo ha pagdudurog han kidney stones ngan pagtambal han urinary tract infections.

Bayabas.name: Bayabas
Bayabas.description: Bayabas (Guava) is a small evergreen tree or shrub that grows up to 10 meters tall. It has smooth, oblong to elliptic leaves with prominent veins. The bark is smooth and peels off in thin flakes. It produces white flowers and yellow-green edible fruits with pink or white flesh.
preparation_steps: For diarrhea: Boil 10 fresh leaves in 2 cups of water for 10 minutes
For wound wash: Boil a handful of leaves until water turns brown
Cool and use to wash affected area 2-3 times daily
For mouthwash: Gargle with cooled boiled leaf water
Bayabas.cultural_relevance: Bayabas is ubiquitous in the Philippines, found in almost every backyard. It is one of the most important traditional medicines, especially for treating diarrhea in children. The leaves are readily available year-round, making it an accessible first-aid remedy in rural areas.
Bayabas.detailed_description: Bayabas or Guava (Psidium guajava) is endorsed by the Philippine DOH as an effective treatment for diarrhea and amebiasis. The leaves contain tannins that provide antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects.

waray.description: An Bayabas (Guava) in usa nga gagmay nga evergreen nga kahoy o shrub nga nagtubo tubtob 10 metros kahitaas. Mayda ini nga maupay, oblong ngan elliptic nga mga bulong nga may prominent nga mga vein. An bark in maupay ngan nag-peel off ha thin flakes. Ini nagprodyus hin mga pula nga burak ngan yellow-green nga edible nga mga prutas nga may pula o puti nga flesh.
medicinal_uses: Ginpapahimuot an toothache (ngumon hin mga bulong)
Ginpaayo an mga wound ngan skin infections
preparation_steps: Para ha diarrhea: Kalad-an an 10 fresh nga mga bulong ha 2 nga baso hin tubig para ha 10 minutos
Para ha wound wash: Kalad-an an usa nga damo hin mga bulong tubtob mag-brown an tubig
Malip-ot ngan gamita para paghugas han affected area 2-3 nga beses ha adlaw
Para ha mouthwash: Gargle nga may malip-ot nga kalad-an nga tubig han mga bulong
waray.cultural_relevance: An Bayabas in ubiquitous ha Pilipinas, mahiagi ha haros tagsa nga backyard. Ini in usa ha mga importante nga traditional nga tambal, labi na ha pagtambal han diarrhea ha mga bata. An mga bulong in ready nga available year-round, nagpapabilin hini komo usa nga accessible nga first-aid remedy ha rural nga mga lugar.
waray.detailed_description: An Bayabas o Guava (Psidium guajava) in gin-endorso han Philippine DOH komo usa nga epektibo nga tambal para ha diarrhea ngan amebiasis. An mga bulong in naglalaman hin tannins nga naghatag hin antimicrobial ngan anti-inflammatory nga mga epekto.

Ampalaya.name: Ampalaya
Ampalaya.description: Ampalaya is a climbing vine with stems that grow up to 5 meters long. It has long, flat, grass-like leaves and produces small white flowers. The bulb consists of 4-20 cloves enclosed in a white or pinkish papery skin. It has a pungent, characteristic odor.
preparation_steps: Wash and slice fresh leaves or fruit
Boil 1 cup of sliced leaves in 2 cups water for 15 minutes
Alternatively, juice fresh leaves and mix with water
Drink 1/2 cup, twice daily before meals
Ampalaya.cultural_relevance: Ampalaya is a staple vegetable in Filipino cuisine and medicine. The Department of Health recognizes its anti-diabetic properties. Despite its bitter taste, it is valued for its numerous health benefits and is often used in the traditional dish 'ginisang ampalaya.'
Ampalaya.detailed_description: Ampalaya or Bitter Melon (Momordica charantia) is recognized by Philippine DOH for its anti-diabetic properties. It contains charantin and momordicin which help lower blood glucose levels.

waray.description: An Ampalaya in usa nga climbing vine nga may mga stems nga nagtubo tubtob 5 metros kahilaba. Mayda ini nga mga naglaba, flat, grass-like nga mga bulong ngan nagprodyus hin mga gagmay nga pula nga mga burak. An bulb in naglalaman hin 4-20 nga cloves nga na-enclose ha usa nga puti o pinkish papery skin. Mayda ini nga pungent, characteristic nga odor.
preparation_steps: Hugasan ngan hiwaan an fresh nga mga bulong o prutas
Kalad-an an 1 nga baso hin gihawa nga mga bulong ha 2 nga baso tubig para ha 15 minutos
Kundi man, i-juice an fresh nga mga bulong ngan ihalad hin tubig
Inumon 1/2 nga baso, duha nga beses ha adlaw before meals
waray.cultural_relevance: An Ampalaya in usa nga staple vegetable ha Filipino cuisine ngan tambal. An Department of Health in ginkilala an iya anti-diabetic nga mga kabtang. Bisan an iya pait nga taste, ginkikilala ini para ha iya damo nga health benefits ngan agsob gingagamit ha traditional nga dish 'ginisang ampalaya.'
waray.detailed_description: An Ampalaya o Bitter Melon (Momordica charantia) in ginkilala han Philippine DOH para ha iya anti-diabetic nga mga kabtang. Ini in naglalaman hin charantin ngan momordicin nga nagtulong pagbaba han blood glucose levels.

Malunggay.name: Malunggay
Malunggay.description: Malunggay is a fast-growing, drought-resistant tree that can reach up to 10 meters in height. It has compound leaves with small oval leaflets, white fragrant flowers, and long, slender seed pods (drumsticks). Every part of the tree is edible and nutritious.
medicinal_uses: Boosts milk production for nursing mothers
Rich source of vitamins and minerals
preparation_steps: Collect fresh leaves and tender stems
Add to soups, stews, or sauté with garlic
For tea: Steep fresh or dried leaves in hot water for 5 minutes
Malunggay.cultural_relevance: Malunggay is called the 'miracle tree' and 'nature's medicine cabinet.' It is a staple in Filipino kitchens, used in tinola, ginataan, and various dishes. Its nutritional value makes it essential for combating malnutrition in rural communities.
Malunggay.detailed_description: Malunggay (Moringa oleifera) is called 'Miracle Tree' and is endorsed by DOH as a nutritional supplement. It contains 7x more Vitamin C than oranges and 4x more Vitamin A than carrots.

waray.description: An Malunggay in usa nga fast-growing, drought-resistant nga kahoy nga maabot tubtob 10 metros kahitaas. Mayda ini nga compound nga mga bulong nga may mga gagmay nga oval nga leaflets, pula nga fragrant nga mga burak, ngan mga naglaba, slender nga seed pods (drumsticks). Tagsa nga parte han kahoy in edible ngan nutritious.
medicinal_uses: Ginpapadako an milk production para ha nursing mothers
Rich source hin vitamins ngan minerals
preparation_steps: Kolektaon an fresh nga mga bulong ngan tender stems
Hugasan nga maupay ha clean nga tubig
Idugang ha soups, stews, o sauté nga may garlic
Para ha tsaa: Steep an fresh o dried nga mga bulong ha mainit nga tubig para ha 5 minutos
waray.cultural_relevance: An Malunggay in tawag nga 'miracle tree' ngan 'nature's medicine cabinet.' Ini in usa nga staple ha Filipino kitchens, gingagamit ha tinola, ginataan, ngan mga dirudilain nga dishes. An iya nutritional value nagpapabilin hini nga importante para ha paglaban han malnutrition ha rural nga mga komunidad.
waray.detailed_description: An Malunggay (Moringa oleifera) in tawag nga 'Miracle Tree' ngan gin-endorso han DOH komo usa nga nutritional supplement. Ini in naglalaman hin 7x nga mas dako an Vitamin C kumpara sa oranges ngan 4x nga mas dako an Vitamin A kumpara sa carrots.

Niyog-niyogan.name: Niyog-niyogan
Niyog-niyogan.description: Niyog-niyogan is a climbing vine with reddish-brown stems that can reach up to 8 meters. It produces elliptical leaves and showy fragrant flowers that change color from white to pink to red. The fruit is oblong and turns brown when mature.
Niyog-niyogan.cultural_relevance: Niyog-niyogan is primarily known as a deworming remedy in the Philippines. The plant is often grown as an ornamental vine due to its beautiful changing-color flowers.
Niyog-niyogan.detailed_description: Niyog-niyogan (Quisqualis indica) is traditionally used as an anthelmintic to expel intestinal worms. The seeds contain quisqualic acid which is effective against Ascaris and other parasitic worms.

waray.description: An Niyog-niyogan in usa nga climbing vine nga may reddish-brown nga mga stems nga maabot tubtob 8 metros. Ini nagprodyus hin elliptic nga mga bulong ngan showy fragrant nga mga burak nga nagbalyo hin color tikang ha pula ngan pula ngan pula. An prutas in oblong ngan nagb brown pag mature.
preparation_steps: Kolektaon an mature nga mga seeds
Kuhaon 1-2 nga kernels nga may tubig
waray.cultural_relevance: An Niyog-niyogan in principalmente ginkilala komo usa nga deworming remedy ha Pilipinas. An tanom in agsob gintanom komo usa nga ornamental vine tungod han iya magupay nga changing-color nga mga burak.
waray.detailed_description: An Niyog-niyogan (Quisqualis indica) in gingagamit ha traditional nga paagi komo usa nga anthelmintic para pagpalayas han intestinal worms. An mga seeds in naglalaman hin quisqualic acid nga epektibo laban ha Ascaris ngan iba nga parasitic worms.

Pansit-pansitan.name: Pansit-pansitan
Pansit-pansitan.description: Pansit-pansitan is a small, succulent annual herb that grows 15-45 cm tall. It has light green, fleshy, translucent leaves with heart-shaped bases. It thrives in damp, shaded areas.
preparation_steps: May be eaten raw in salads
For tea: Boil 1 cup in 2 cups water
Pansit-pansitan.cultural_relevance: Pansit-pansitan is commonly found in damp corners of Filipino homes. It is valued for treating gout and arthritis and is often eaten as a salad vegetable.
Pansit-pansitan.detailed_description: Pansit-pansitan (Peperomia pellucida) is traditionally used for treating arthritis, gout, and kidney stones. It has diuretic and anti-inflammatory properties that help lower uric acid levels.

waray.description: An Pansit-pansitan in usa nga gagmay, succulent annual herb nga nagtubo 15-45 cm kahitaas. Mayda ini nga light green, fleshy, translucent nga mga bulong nga may heart-shaped nga mga base. Nagtubo ini ha damp, shaded nga mga lugar.
preparation_steps: Hugasan an fresh whole nga tanom
Puede nga kaonon ha raw ha salads
Para ha tsaa: Kalad-an an 1 nga baso ha 2 nga baso tubig
Inumon 1 nga baso, 3 nga beses ha adlaw
waray.cultural_relevance: An Pansit-pansitan in komun nga mahiagi ha damp nga mga corner han Filipino homes. Ginkikilala ini para ha pagtambal han gout ngan arthritis ngan agsob in kaonon komo usa nga salad vegetable.
waray.detailed_description: An Pansit-pansitan (Peperomia pellucida) in gingagamit ha traditional nga paagi para ha pagtambal han arthritis, gout, ngan kidney stones. Mayda ini diuretic ngan anti-inflammatory nga mga kabtang nga nagtulong pagbaba han uric acid levels.

Yerba Buena.name: Yerba Buena
Yerba Buena.description: Yerba Buena is a perennial creeping herb with hairy, square stems. It has small, oval toothed leaves that emit a strong minty aroma when crushed. It produces small white or purple flowers.
Yerba Buena.cultural_relevance: Yerba Buena (good herb) has been used by Filipino families for generations as a pain reliever. Its minty aroma makes it pleasant to use.
Yerba Buena.detailed_description: Yerba Buena (Clinopodium douglasii) is one of the ten DOH-endorsed medicinal plants for pain relief. Its menthol content provides effective relief from body aches, headaches, and menstrual cramps.

waray.description: An Yerba Buena in usa nga perennial creeping herb nga may hairy, square nga mga stems. Mayda ini nga mga gagmay, oval toothed nga mga bulong nga nagpapakita hin strong minty aroma pagkatapos gindurog. Ini nagprodyus hin mga gagmay nga pula o lila nga mga burak.
preparation_steps: Kolektaon an fresh nga mga bulong
Kalad-an an 1 nga baso ha 2 nga baso tubig
Para ha inhalation: Inhale an steam
I-apply directly ha mga sakit nga lugar
waray.cultural_relevance: An Yerba Buena (maupay nga herb) in gingagamit han Filipino families para ha mga henerasyon komo usa nga pain reliever. An iya minty aroma nagpapahimuot hini nga maupay gamiton.
waray.detailed_description: An Yerba Buena (Clinopodium douglasii) in usa ha mga napulo nga DOH-endorsed nga medicinal nga tanom para ha pain relief. An iya menthol content in naghatag hin epektibo nga relief tikang ha body aches, headaches, ngan menstrual cramps.

Bawang.name: Bawang
Bawang.description: Bawang is a bulbous perennial plant that grows up to 1 meter tall. It has long, flat leaves and produces small white flowers. The bulb has 4-20 cloves with a pungent odor.
preparation_steps: Swallow with water or mix with honey
Bawang.cultural_relevance: Bawang is indispensable in Filipino cooking and medicine. Every Filipino kitchen has garlic, used both as spice and medicine.
Bawang.detailed_description: Bawang or Garlic (Allium sativum) is recognized by the DOH for its cardiovascular benefits. It contains allicin which helps lower blood pressure, reduce cholesterol, and prevent blood clot formation.

waray.description: An Bawang in usa nga bulbous perennial nga tanom nga nagtubo tubtob 1 metro kahitaas. Mayda ini nga mga naglaba, flat nga mga bulong ngan nagprodyus hin mga gagmay nga pula nga mga burak. An bulb in may 4-20 nga cloves nga may pungent nga odor.
preparation_steps: Peel ngan gindurog an 2-3 nga cloves
Inumon nga may tubig o ihalad hin honey
waray.cultural_relevance: An Bawang in indispensable ha Filipino cooking ngan tambal. Tagsa nga Filipino kitchen in may garlic, gingagamit pareho komo spice ngan tambal.
waray.detailed_description: An Bawang o Garlic (Allium sativum) in ginkilala han DOH para ha iya cardiovascular nga mga benefits. Ini in naglalaman hin allicin nga nagtulong pagbaba han blood pressure, pagpaka-ubos han cholesterol, ngan pagprevenir han blood clot formation.

Akapulko.name: Akapulko
Akapulko.description: Akapulko is a coarse erect shrub that grows 1-3 meters tall. It has bright yellow flowers arranged in a candelabra shape. The leaves are compound with 8-12 leaflets.
preparation_steps: For bath: Add to bath water
Akapulko.cultural_relevance: Akapulko is the go-to remedy for fungal skin infections. Its distinctive yellow flowers make it easy to identify.
Akapulko.detailed_description: Akapulko or Ringworm Bush (Senna alata) is DOH-endorsed for treating fungal skin infections. The leaves contain chrysophanic acid which is effective against ringworm, scabies, and eczema.

waray.description: An Akapulko in usa nga coarse erect shrub nga nagtubo 1-3 metros kahitaas. Mayda ini nga bright yellow nga mga burak nga na-arrange ha candelabra shape. An mga bulong in compound nga may 8-12 nga leaflets.
preparation_steps: Kolektaon an fresh mature nga mga bulong
Para ha bath: Idugang ha bath tubig
waray.cultural_relevance: An Akapulko in an go-to remedy para ha fungal skin infections. An iya distinctive yellow nga mga burak nagpapadali hini nga ma-ila.
waray.detailed_description: An Akapulko o Ringworm Bush (Senna alata) in DOH-endorsed para ha pagtambal han fungal skin infections. An mga bulong in naglalaman hin chrysophanic acid nga epektibo laban ha ringworm, scabies, ngan eczema.

Tsaang Gubat.name: Tsaang Gubat
Tsaang Gubat.description: Tsaang Gubat is a shrub that grows 1-3 meters tall. It has small, oval leaves and produces small white flowers. It is commonly found in forests and wild areas.
Tsaang Gubat.cultural_relevance: Tsaang Gubat is used as a tea substitute and for treating stomach ailments in rural communities.
Tsaang Gubat.detailed_description: Tsaang Gubat (Ehretia microphylla) is endorsed by the Philippine DOH for treating diarrhea and stomach problems. It has anti-spasmodic and anti-diarrheal properties.

waray.description: An Tsaang Gubat in usa nga shrub nga nagtubo 1-3 metros kahitaas. Mayda ini nga mga gagmay, oval nga mga bulong ngan nagprodyus hin mga gagmay nga pula nga mga burak. Ini in komun nga mahiagi ha forests ngan wild nga mga lugar.
preparation_steps: Kolektaon an fresh nga mga bulong
waray.cultural_relevance: An Tsaang Gubat in gingagamit komo usa nga tsaa substitute ngan para ha pagtambal han stomach ailments ha rural nga mga komunidad.
waray.detailed_description: An Tsaang Gubat (Ehretia microphylla) in gin-endorso han Philippine DOH para ha pagtambal han diarrhea ngan stomach problems. Mayda ini anti-spasmodic ngan anti-diarrheal nga mga kabtang.

Alagaw.name: Alagaw
Alagaw.description: Alagaw is a shrub or small tree with fragrant leaves. It grows in forests and is used for respiratory ailments.
Alagaw.cultural_relevance: Alagaw is used in traditional medicine for respiratory problems.
Alagaw.detailed_description: Alagaw (Premna odorata) is a medicinal plant traditionally used in Philippine traditional medicine. It is known for its medicinal properties including Treats cough, Relieves asthma, Expectorant. This plant has been used by Filipino communities for generations and continues to be an important part of traditional healing practices.

waray.description: An Alagaw in usa nga shrub o gagmay nga kahoy nga may fragrant nga mga bulong. Nagtubo ini ha forests ngan gingagamit para ha respiratory ailments.
waray.cultural_relevance: An Alagaw in gingagamit ha traditional nga tambal para ha respiratory problems.
waray.detailed_description: An Alagaw (Premna odorata) in usa nga medicinal nga tanom nga gingagamit ha traditional nga paagi ha Philippine traditional nga tambal. Ginkilala ini para ha iya medicinal nga mga kabtag upod an Treats cough, Relieves asthma, Expectorant. Ini nga tanom in gingagamit han Filipino communities para ha mga henerasyon ngan nagpapabilin nga importante nga parte han traditional healing practices.

Avocado.name: Avocado
Avocado.description: Avocado is an evergreen tree that produces nutrient-dense fruits with creamy texture. The leaves are also used medicinally for kidney stones and respiratory problems.
medicinal_uses: Rich in healthy fats and nutrients
preparation_steps: For leaves tea: Collect young leaves, wash thoroughly
Boil leaves in water for 10-15 minutes
Avocado.cultural_relevance: Avocado is popular worldwide for its creamy, nutritious fruit. The leaves are traditionally used for kidney and respiratory problems.
Avocado.detailed_description: Avocado (Persea americana) is valued for its nutrient-rich fruit containing healthy fats, fiber, and vitamins. The leaves are used in traditional medicine for treating kidney stones and respiratory issues, containing beneficial flavonoids and phenolic compounds.

waray.description: An Avocado in usa nga evergreen nga kahoy nga nagprodyus hin nutrient-dense nga mga prutas nga may creamy texture. An mga bulong in gingagamit liwat nga medicinal para ha kidney stones ngan respiratory problems.
medicinal_uses: Rich ha healthy fats ngan nutrients
preparation_steps: Kaonon an fresh nga prutas para ha nutrition
Para ha bulong tsaa: Kolektaon an mga gagmay nga bulong, hugasan nga maupay
Kalad-an an mga bulong ha tubig para ha 10-15 minutos
Inumon komo tsaa, 2-3 nga beses ha adlaw
waray.cultural_relevance: An Avocado in popular worldwide para ha iya creamy, nutritious nga prutas. An mga bulong in gingagamit ha traditional nga paagi para ha kidney ngan respiratory problems.
waray.detailed_description: An Avocado (Persea americana) in ginkikilala para ha iya nutrient-rich nga prutas nga naglalaman hin healthy fats, fiber, ngan vitamins. An mga bulong in gingagamit ha traditional nga tambal para ha pagtambal han kidney stones ngan respiratory issues, naglalaman hin beneficial flavonoids ngan phenolic compounds.

Basil.name: Basil
Basil.description: Basil is an aromatic herb with oval leaves. It is widely used in cooking and traditional medicine.
Basil.cultural_relevance: Balanoi is used in Filipino cooking and for treating cough and colds.
Basil.detailed_description: Basil (Ocimum basilicum) is a medicinal plant traditionally used in Philippine traditional medicine. It is known for its medicinal properties including Relieves cough, Digestive aid, Anti-stress. This plant has been used by Filipino communities for generations and continues to be an important part of traditional healing practices.

waray.description: An Basil in usa nga aromatic herb nga may oval nga mga bulong. Ini in haluag nga gingagamit ha cooking ngan traditional nga tambal.
preparation_steps: Himo tsaa nga may mga bulong
waray.cultural_relevance: An Balanoi in gingagamit ha Filipino cooking ngan para ha pagtambal han ubo ngan sip-on.
waray.detailed_description: An Basil (Ocimum basilicum) in usa nga medicinal nga tanom nga gingagamit ha traditional nga paagi ha Philippine traditional nga tambal. Ginkilala ini para ha iya medicinal nga mga kabtang upod an Relieves cough, Digestive aid, Anti-stress. Ini nga tanom in gingagamit han Filipino communities para ha mga henerasyon ngan nagpapabilin nga importante nga parte han traditional healing practices.

Bignay.name: Bignay
Bignay.description: Bignay is a small tree with leathery leaves. It produces small red fruits that are edible and medicinal.
Bignay.cultural_relevance: Bignay fruits are eaten fresh and used in traditional medicine for fever.
Bignay.detailed_description: Bignay (Antidesma bunius) is a medicinal plant traditionally used in Philippine traditional medicine. It is known for its medicinal properties including Treats fever, Anti-inflammatory, Rich in antioxidants. This plant has been used by Filipino communities for generations and continues to be an important part of traditional healing practices.

waray.description: An Bignay in usa nga gagmay nga kahoy nga may leathery nga mga bulong. Ini nagprodyus hin mga gagmay nga pula nga mga prutas nga edible ngan medicinal.
preparation_steps: Kalad-an an mga bulong o bark
Kaonon an fresh nga mga prutas

Oregano.name: Oregano
Oregano.description: Oregano is a succulent herb with fleshy leaves and strong aroma. It is different from Mediterranean oregano.
Oregano.cultural_relevance: Oregano is a popular home remedy for cough and asthma in the Philippines.
Oregano.detailed_description: Oregano (Coleus amboinicus) is a medicinal plant traditionally used in Philippine traditional medicine. It is known for its medicinal properties including Relieves cough, Treats colds, Anti-asthmatic. This plant has been used by Filipino communities for generations and continues to be an important part of traditional healing practices.

waray.description: An Oregano in usa nga succulent herb nga may fleshy nga mga bulong ngan strong aroma. Ini in iba tikang ha Mediterranean oregano.
waray.cultural_relevance: An Oregano in usa nga popular nga home remedy para ha ubo ngan asthma ha Pilipinas.
waray.detailed_description: An Oregano (Coleus amboinicus) in usa nga medicinal nga tanom nga gingagamit ha traditional nga paagi ha Philippine traditional nga tambal. Ginkilala ini para ha iya medicinal nga mga kabtang upod an Relieves cough, Treats colds, Anti-asthmatic. Ini nga tanom in gingagamit han Filipino communities para ha mga henerasyon ngan nagpapabilin nga importante nga parte han traditional healing practices.

Pandan.name: Pandan
Pandan.description: Pandan is a plant with long, narrow leaves. It is widely used for its fragrant aroma in cooking.
Pandan.cultural_relevance: Pandan is essential in Filipino cooking for its aromatic flavor.
Pandan.detailed_description: Pandan (Pandanus amaryllifolius) is a medicinal plant traditionally used in Philippine traditional medicine. It is known for its medicinal properties including Mild laxative, Lowers blood pressure, Anti-rheumatic. This plant has been used by Filipino communities for generations and continues to be an important part of traditional healing practices.

waray.description: An Pandan in usa nga tanom nga may darudako, narit nga mga bulong. Ini in haluag nga gingagamit para ha iya fragrant aroma ha cooking.
preparation_steps: Tie an mga bulong ha knot
waray.cultural_relevance: An Pandan in esensyal ha Filipino cooking para ha iya aromatic flavor.
waray.detailed_description: An Pandan (Pandanus amaryllifolius) in usa nga medicinal nga tanom nga gingagamit ha traditional nga paagi ha Philippine traditional nga tambal. Ginkilala ini para ha iya medicinal nga mga kabtang upod an Mild laxative, Lowers blood pressure, Anti-rheumatic. Ini nga tanom in gingagamit han Filipino communities para ha mga henerasyon ngan nagpapabilin nga importante nga parte han traditional healing practices.

Papaya.name: Papaya
Papaya.description: Papaya is a tree with large, palmately lobed leaves. The fruit and leaves are both medicinal.
Papaya.cultural_relevance: Papaya is widely consumed and used for digestive health.
Papaya.detailed_description: Papaya (Carica papaya) contains papain enzyme that aids digestion and helps break down proteins. The fruit and leaves are both medicinal and used traditionally for digestive health.

waray.description: An Papaya in usa nga kahoy nga may dako, palmately lobed nga mga bulong. An prutas ngan mga bulong in pareho medicinal.
preparation_steps: Kalad-an an mga bulong para ha tsaa
waray.cultural_relevance: An Papaya in haluag nga ginkaon ngan gingagamit para ha digestive health.
waray.detailed_description: An Papaya (Carica papaya) in nagpapakita hin papain enzyme nga nagtulong ha digestion ngan natutulungan pag-break down hin proteins. An prutas ngan mga bulong in pareho medicinal ngan gingagamit ha traditional nga paagi para ha digestive health.

Tanglad.name: Tanglad
Tanglad.description: Tanglad is a tall perennial grass with long, slender leaves and a strong lemon scent. It grows in clumps up to 2 meters tall and is widely cultivated throughout the Philippines.
preparation_steps: Wash fresh stalks and leaves thoroughly
Boil 2-3 stalks in 3 cups water for 10 minutes
Tanglad.cultural_relevance: Tanglad is essential in Filipino cuisine, particularly in sinigang and various meat dishes. It is also widely used as a traditional remedy for fever and digestive problems in Calbayog and throughout Samar.
Tanglad.detailed_description: Tanglad or Lemongrass (Cymbopogon citratus) is widely used in Philippine traditional medicine for its calming, digestive, and fever-reducing properties. It contains citral and geraniol which have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects.

waray.description: An Tanglad in usa nga haluag nga perennial grass nga may darudako, slender nga mga bulong ngan usa nga strong lemon scent. Ini in magtubo ha clumps tubtob 2 meters nga kahitas ngan haluag nga gintatanim ha bug-os nga Pilipinas.
preparation_steps: Hugasan an fresh nga mga stalks ngan mga bulong nga maupay
Kalad-an 2-3 stalks ha 3 cups tubig para ha 10 minutos
Inumon komo tsaa, 2-3 beses ha adlaw
waray.cultural_relevance: An Tanglad in esensyal ha Filipino cuisine, labi na ha sinigang ngan damo nga meat dishes. Ini gihapon in haluag nga gingagamit komo usa nga traditional remedy para ha fever ngan digestive problems ha Calbayog ngan bug-os nga Samar.
waray.detailed_description: An Tanglad o Lemongrass (Cymbopogon citratus) in haluag nga gingagamit ha Philippine traditional nga tambal para ha iya calming, digestive, ngan fever-reducing properties. Ini in nagpapakita hin citral ngan geraniol nga may antimicrobial ngan anti-inflammatory effects.

Atis.name: Atis
Atis.description: Atis is a small deciduous tree with oblong leaves that produces sweet, creamy fruits with a distinctive scaly green skin. It grows well in tropical climates and is common in backyards across Samar.
preparation_steps: Boil 1 cup leaves in 2 cups water for 15 minutes
Strain and drink warm, 2-3 times daily
Atis.cultural_relevance: Atis is a favorite backyard fruit tree in Calbayog and throughout the Visayas. The leaves have been used traditionally as a fever remedy for children, and the sweet fruit is enjoyed by all ages.
Atis.detailed_description: Atis (Annona squamosa) leaves are traditionally used in Philippine folk medicine for treating fever, diarrhea, and intestinal parasites. The fruit is rich in Vitamin C and dietary fiber.

waray.description: An Atis in usa nga gagmay nga deciduous tree nga may oblong nga mga bulong nga nagbubuhat hin sweet, creamy nga mga prutas nga may distinctive scaly green nga skin. Ini in maupay magtubo ha tropical climates ngan common ha backyards bug-os han Samar.
preparation_steps: Kuhaon an fresh nga mga bulong
Kalad-an 1 cup mga bulong ha 2 cups tubig para ha 15 minutos
Strain ngan inumon nga painit, 2-3 beses ha adlaw
waray.cultural_relevance: An Atis in paborito nga backyard fruit tree ha Calbayog ngan bug-os han Visayas. An mga bulong in gingagamit ha traditional nga paagi komo fever remedy para ha mga bata, ngan an sweet nga prutas in ginhahalagad han tanan nga edad.
waray.detailed_description: An Atis (Annona squamosa) nga mga bulong in gingagamit ha traditional nga paagi ha Philippine folk medicine para ha pagtambal hin fever, diarrhea, ngan intestinal parasites. An prutas in rich ha Vitamin C ngan dietary fiber.

Gabi.name: Gabi
Gabi.description: Gabi is a perennial herbaceous plant with large, heart-shaped leaves that can grow up to 1 meter long. The starchy corms and edible leaves are important food sources in the Philippines.
preparation_steps: For food: Peel corms, boil thoroughly to remove oxalates
For insect bites: Crush fresh leaves and apply to affected area
For tea: Boil clean leaves briefly, strain and drink
Gabi.cultural_relevance: Gabi is a staple root crop in rural Samar and Calbayog. The leaves (laing) are cooked with coconut milk in the famous Bicol dish, though this preparation is also popular in Samar. It represents resilience as it grows well in wet conditions.
Gabi.detailed_description: Gabi or Taro (Colocasia esculenta) is an important root crop in the Philippines. The corms must be cooked thoroughly to remove calcium oxalate crystals. The leaves are used externally for insect bites and internally as a vegetable.

waray.description: An Gabi in usa nga perennial herbaceous plant nga may dako, heart-shaped nga mga bulong nga pwede magtubo tubtob 1 meter nga kahilaba. An starchy corms ngan edible nga mga bulong in importante nga food sources ha Pilipinas.
medicinal_uses: Ginpapabati an insect bites (crushed nga mga bulong)
preparation_steps: Para ha pagkaon: Peel an corms, kalad-an nga maupay para pagpalayas hin oxalates
Para ha insect bites: Gindurog an fresh nga mga bulong ngan i-apply ha affected area
Para ha tsaa: Kalad-an an limpyo nga mga bulong nga madali, strain ngan inumon
waray.cultural_relevance: An Gabi in usa nga staple root crop ha rural Samar ngan Calbayog. An mga bulong (laing) in ginaluto nga may coconut milk ha famous nga Bicol dish, bisan nga ini nga preparation in popular gihap ha Samar. Ini in nerepresenta hin resilience kay maupay ini magtubo ha wet conditions.
waray.detailed_description: An Gabi o Taro (Colocasia esculenta) in importante nga root crop ha Pilipinas. An mga corms kinahanglan lutoon nga maupay para pagpalayas hin calcium oxalate crystals. An mga bulong in gingagamit ha laog para ha insect bites ngan ha sulod komo vegetable.

Sampaguita.name: Sampaguita
Sampaguita.description: Sampaguita is a small shrub with fragrant white flowers that bloom year-round. It has glossy green leaves and is the national flower of the Philippines. It grows abundantly in warm, tropical climates like Calbayog.
preparation_steps: For tea: Steep fresh flowers in hot water for 5 minutes
For headache: Crush flowers and apply to temples
For eye wash: Boil flowers, cool and use as gentle eye rinse
For aroma: String flowers into garlands for natural air freshener
Sampaguita.cultural_relevance: Sampaguita is the national flower of the Philippines and holds deep cultural significance. In Calbayog, it is commonly used in religious offerings, worn as garlands (sampaguita necklaces), and grown in home gardens for its fragrance and beauty.
Sampaguita.detailed_description: Sampaguita (Jasminum sambac) is the Philippines' national flower. Its fragrant blooms are used in traditional medicine for headaches, relaxation, and as an aromatic remedy for stress and anxiety.

waray.description: An Sampaguita in usa nga gagmay nga shrub nga may fragrant white nga mga burak nga nagbuburak year-round. Mayda ini glossy green nga mga bulong ngan ini in an national flower han Pilipinas. Ini in magtubo abundantly ha warm, tropical climates sugad ha Calbayog.
medicinal_uses: Ginpapahimuot an mga sakit han ulo
Gingagamit komo poultice para ha mga wound
preparation_steps: Para ha tsaa: Steep an fresh nga mga burak ha hot water para ha 5 minutos
Para ha sakit han ulo: Gindurog an mga burak ngan i-apply ha temples
Para ha eye wash: Kalad-an an mga burak, painiton ngan gamita komo gentle eye rinse
Para ha aroma: I-string an mga burak ha garlands para ha natural air freshener
waray.cultural_relevance: An Sampaguita in an national flower han Pilipinas ngan mayda deep cultural significance. Ha Calbayog, ini in common nga gingagamit ha religious offerings, ginsusuot komo garlands (sampaguita necklaces), ngan gintatanim ha home gardens para ha iya fragrance ngan beauty.
waray.detailed_description: An Sampaguita (Jasminum sambac) in an national flower han Pilipinas. An iya fragrant blooms in gingagamit ha traditional nga tambal para ha mga sakit han ulo, relaxation, ngan komo usa nga aromatic remedy para ha stress ngan anxiety.

Lanzones.name: Lanzones
Lanzones.description: Lanzones is a medium-sized fruit tree that produces clusters of sweet-sour yellowish fruits with translucent flesh. It has pinnate leaves and thrives in tropical climates, particularly in Southern Leyte and nearby areas like Calbayog.
preparation_steps: For fever: Boil bark in water and drink as decoction
For mosquito repellent: Burn dried lanzones peels
For diarrhea: Boil fruit peels and drink liquid
Eat fresh fruit for vitamin C and antioxidants
Lanzones.cultural_relevance: Lanzones is celebrated in the Camiguin Lanzones Festival and is common in nearby regions including parts of Samar. In Calbayog, the fruit is enjoyed during harvest season and the tree provides shade in rural communities.
Lanzones.detailed_description: Lanzones (Lansium parasiticum) is a popular tropical fruit native to Southeast Asia. The bark has traditional uses for fever and malaria, while the fruit is rich in vitamins and antioxidants.

waray.description: An Lanzones in usa nga medium-sized fruit tree nga nagbubuhat hin clusters hin sweet-sour yellowish nga mga prutas nga may translucent flesh. Mayda ini pinnate nga mga bulong ngan maupay magtubo ha tropical climates, labi na ha Southern Leyte ngan mga harani nga lugar sugad ha Calbayog.
medicinal_uses: Ginapaway an mga mosquitoes (pag-sunog hin dried peels)
preparation_steps: Para ha fever: Kalad-an an bark ha tubig ngan inumon komo decoction
Para ha mosquito repellent: Sunugon an dried nga lanzones peels
Para ha diarrhea: Kalad-an an fruit peels ngan inumon an liquid
Kaonon an fresh nga prutas para ha vitamin C ngan antioxidants
waray.cultural_relevance: An Lanzones in gincelebrate ha Camiguin Lanzones Festival ngan common ha harani nga mga rehiyon upod an mga parte han Samar. Ha Calbayog, an prutas in ginhahalagad durante han harvest season ngan an kahoy in naghatag hin shade ha rural communities.
waray.detailed_description: An Lanzones (Lansium parasiticum) in usa nga popular tropical fruit native ha Southeast Asia. An bark in mayda traditional uses para ha fever ngan malaria, samtang an prutas in rich ha vitamins ngan antioxidants.

Balimbing.name: Balimbing
Balimbing.description: Balimbing is a small tropical tree with compound leaves that produces distinctive star-shaped yellow fruits when sliced. The fruits are crisp, juicy, and range from sour to sweet depending on variety.
preparation_steps: Eat fresh ripe fruit for vitamins
For cough: Boil unripe fruit and drink juice
For high blood pressure: Drink fresh juice regularly
Add to salads for digestive health
Balimbing.cultural_relevance: Balimbing trees are common in backyards throughout Calbayog and Samar. The sour variety is used in cooking (sinigang, paksiw) while the sweet variety is eaten fresh. The star-shaped slices make it a decorative garnish for drinks and dishes.
Balimbing.detailed_description: Balimbing or Star Fruit (Averrhoa carambola) is valued for its distinctive shape and health benefits. It contains oxalates and should be consumed in moderation by those with kidney issues. Rich in Vitamin C and antioxidants.

waray.description: An Balimbing in usa nga gagmay nga tropical tree nga may compound nga mga bulong nga nagbubuhat hin distinctive star-shaped yellow nga mga prutas nga gin-hiwa. An mga prutas in crisp, juicy, ngan nagkakahalo tikang ha sour tubtob sweet depende ha variety.
preparation_steps: Kaonon an fresh ripe nga prutas para ha vitamins
Para ha ubo: Kalad-an an unripe nga prutas ngan inumon an juice
Para ha high blood pressure: Inumon an fresh juice nga regular
Idugang ha salads para ha digestive health
waray.cultural_relevance: An Balimbing nga mga kahoy in common ha backyards bug-os han Calbayog ngan Samar. An sour nga variety in gingagamit ha cooking (sinigang, paksiw) samtang an sweet variety in ginkaon nga fresh. An star-shaped nga hiwa in naghihimo hini nga decorative garnish para ha drinks ngan dishes.
waray.detailed_description: An Balimbing o Star Fruit (Averrhoa carambola) in ginkikilala para ha iya distinctive shape ngan health benefits. Ini in nagpapakita hin oxalates ngan kinahanglan konsumon ha moderation han mga mayda kidney issues. Rich ha Vitamin C ngan antioxidants.

Kalabasa.name: Kalabasa
Kalabasa.description: Kalabasa is a vine plant with large, lobed leaves and yellow-orange flowers. It produces large, round to elongated fruits with orange flesh and numerous seeds. It thrives in tropical climates and is extensively grown in Calbayog.
preparation_steps: Cook flesh in various dishes (ginataan, soup)
Boil young leaves and flowers as vegetable
Roast seeds and eat as deworming remedy
Make soup with flowers for respiratory health
Kalabasa.cultural_relevance: Kalabasa is a staple vegetable in Calbayog cuisine, used in ginataang kalabasa, soups, and various traditional dishes. The flowers (bulaklak ng kalabasa) are highly prized as a vegetable and are believed to be good for the lungs.
Kalabasa.detailed_description: Kalabasa or Squash (Cucurbita maxima) is an important vegetable crop in the Philippines. The orange flesh is rich in beta-carotene and vitamins. The flowers, leaves, and seeds all have traditional medicinal and culinary uses.

waray.description: An Kalabasa in usa nga vine plant nga may dako, lobed nga mga bulong ngan yellow-orange nga mga burak. Ini in nagbubuhat hin dako, round tubtob elongated nga mga prutas nga may orange flesh ngan damo nga seeds. Ini in maupay magtubo ha tropical climates ngan haluag nga gintatanim ha Calbayog.
medicinal_uses: An mga seeds in gingagamit komo dewormer
preparation_steps: Lutoon an flesh ha damo nga dishes (ginataan, soup)
Kalad-an an mga gagmay nga bulong ngan mga burak komo vegetable
I-roast an mga seeds ngan kaonon komo deworming remedy
Himo soup nga may mga burak para ha respiratory health
waray.cultural_relevance: An Kalabasa in usa nga staple vegetable ha Calbayog cuisine, gingagamit ha ginataang kalabasa, soups, ngan damo nga traditional dishes. An mga burak (bulaklak ng kalabasa) in haluag nga ginkikilala komo vegetable ngan ginihingagwaran nga maupay para ha mga baga.
waray.detailed_description: An Kalabasa o Squash (Cucurbita maxima) in importante nga vegetable crop ha Pilipinas. An orange flesh in rich ha beta-carotene ngan vitamins. An mga burak, mga bulong, ngan mga seeds tanan in mayda traditional medicinal ngan culinary uses.

Alugbati.name: Alugbati
Alugbati.description: Alugbati is a fast-growing, soft-stemmed climbing vine with thick, fleshy, heart-shaped leaves. It produces dark purple berries and thrives in hot, humid climates. It is commonly found in gardens throughout Calbayog.
preparation_steps: Add to sinigang or sauté with garlic
Use as substitute for spinach in any dish
Alugbati.cultural_relevance: Alugbati is a common backyard vegetable in Calbayog households. It grows easily without much care and provides nutritious greens year-round. The purple juice from the berries is sometimes used as a natural food coloring.
Alugbati.detailed_description: Alugbati (Basella alba) is a popular leafy vegetable in the Philippines, similar to spinach but more heat-tolerant. It is rich in iron, calcium, and vitamins A and C. The mucilaginous texture is similar to spinach when cooked.

waray.description: An Alugbati in usa nga fast-growing, soft-stemmed climbing vine nga may kapot, fleshy, heart-shaped nga mga bulong. Ini in nagbubuhat hin dark purple nga mga berries ngan maupay magtubo ha hot, humid climates. Ini in common nga makita ha gardens bug-os han Calbayog.
preparation_steps: Hugasan an mga bulong nga maupay
Idugang ha sinigang o sauté nga may garlic
Gamita komo substitute para ha spinach ha bisan ano nga dish
waray.cultural_relevance: An Alugbati in usa nga common backyard vegetable ha Calbayog households. Ini in dali magtubo waray damo nga care ngan naghatag hin nutritious greens year-round. An purple juice tikang ha berries in minsan gingagamit komo natural food coloring.
waray.detailed_description: An Alugbati (Basella alba) in usa nga popular leafy vegetable ha Pilipinas, pareho ha spinach pero mas heat-tolerant. Ini in rich ha iron, calcium, ngan vitamins A ngan C. An mucilaginous texture in pareho ha spinach nga ginaluto.

Kangkong.name: Kangkong
Kangkong.description: Kangkong is a semi-aquatic tropical plant with hollow stems and long, lance-shaped leaves. It grows prolifically in water or moist soil and is one of the most popular leafy vegetables in the Philippines.
preparation_steps: Wash thoroughly (especially if harvested from water)
Sauté with garlic and soy sauce (adobong kangkong)
Add to sinigang as green vegetable
Kangkong.cultural_relevance: Kangkong is the 'poor man's vegetable' in the Philippines due to its affordability and easy growth. In Calbayog, it grows wild in rice paddies, streams, and wetlands. Adobong kangkong is a beloved dish throughout the region.
Kangkong.detailed_description: Kangkong (Ipomoea aquatica) is a popular leafy vegetable in Southeast Asia. It grows rapidly in aquatic or moist environments. Rich in iron, vitamins, and minerals. It is a staple green vegetable in Filipino cuisine.

waray.description: An Kangkong in usa nga semi-aquatic tropical plant nga may hollow stems ngan darudako, lance-shaped nga mga bulong. Ini in magtubo prolifically ha tubig o moist soil ngan in usa ha mga popular nga leafy vegetables ha Pilipinas.
preparation_steps: Hugasan nga maupay (labi na kun ginkuha tikang ha tubig)
Sauté nga may garlic ngan soy sauce (adobong kangkong)
Idugang ha sinigang komo green vegetable
Steam ngan serbisyo nga may bagoong
waray.cultural_relevance: An Kangkong in an 'poor man's vegetable' ha Pilipinas tungod ha iya affordability ngan dali nga pagtubo. Ha Calbayog, ini in magtubo wild ha rice paddies, streams, ngan wetlands. An Adobong kangkong in usa nga beloved dish bug-os han rehiyon.
waray.detailed_description: An Kangkong (Ipomoea aquatica) in usa nga popular leafy vegetable ha Southeast Asia. Ini in magtubo rapidly ha aquatic o moist environments. Rich ha iron, vitamins, ngan minerals. Ini in usa nga staple green vegetable ha Filipino cuisine.

Neem.name: Neem
Neem.description: Neem is a fast-growing tree with medicinal bark, leaves, and seeds. It has been used in traditional medicine for thousands of years.
Neem.cultural_relevance: Neem is revered in traditional medicine and is commonly found in Indian and Southeast Asian communities.
Neem.detailed_description: Neem (Azadirachta indica) is a trees plant traditionally used in Philippine traditional medicine. It is known for its medicinal properties including Treats skin diseases, Antibacterial, Antifungal. This plant has been used by Filipino communities for generations and continues to be an important part of traditional healing practices.

waray.description: An Neem in usa nga fast-growing tree nga may medicinal bark, mga bulong, ngan mga seeds. Ini in gingagamit ha traditional nga tambal para ha mga ribo ka tuig.
preparation_steps: Kalad-an an mga bulong para ha tsaa
Ngangaon an mga twigs para ha dental health
waray.cultural_relevance: An Neem in ginhahangaad ha traditional nga tambal ngan common nga makita ha Indian ngan Southeast Asian communities.
waray.detailed_description: An Neem (Azadirachta indica) in usa nga trees plant nga gingagamit ha traditional nga paagi ha Philippine traditional nga tambal. Ginkilala ini para ha iya medicinal nga mga kabtang upod an Treats skin diseases, Antibacterial, Antifungal. Ini nga tanom in gingagamit han Filipino communities para ha mga henerasyon ngan nagpapabilin nga importante nga parte han traditional healing practices.

Acacia.name: Acacia
Acacia.description: Acacia is a fast-growing tree with yellow flowers and distinctive curved seed pods. Some species have medicinal bark.
Acacia.cultural_relevance: Acacia trees are common in the Philippines and used for various purposes including shade and timber.
Acacia.detailed_description: Acacia (Acacia auriculiformis) is a trees plant traditionally used in Philippine traditional medicine. It is known for its medicinal properties including Treats diarrhea, Sore throat remedy, Antiseptic. This plant has been used by Filipino communities for generations and continues to be an important part of traditional healing practices.

waray.description: An Acacia in usa nga fast-growing tree nga may yellow nga mga burak ngan distinctive curved nga seed pods. An iba nga species in mayda medicinal bark.
preparation_steps: Kalad-an an bark para ha tsaa
Gargle an decoction para ha sore throat
I-apply an bark powder ha mga wound
waray.cultural_relevance: An Acacia nga mga kahoy in common ha Pilipinas ngan gingagamit para ha damo nga mga katuyoan upod an shade ngan timber.
waray.detailed_description: An Acacia (Acacia auriculiformis) in usa nga trees plant nga gingagamit ha traditional nga paagi ha Philippine traditional nga tambal. Ginkilala ini para ha iya medicinal nga mga kabtang upod an Treats diarrhea, Sore throat remedy, Antiseptic. Ini nga tanom in gingagamit han Filipino communities para ha mga henerasyon ngan nagpapabilin nga importante nga parte han traditional healing practices.

Mahogany.name: Mahogany
Mahogany.description: Mahogany is a large tropical tree with compound leaves and valuable hardwood. The bark and seeds have traditional medicinal uses.
Mahogany.cultural_relevance: Mahogany is prized for its timber and widely planted throughout the tropics.
Mahogany.detailed_description: Mahogany (Swietenia macrophylla) is a trees plant traditionally used in Philippine traditional medicine. It is known for its medicinal properties including Treats diarrhea, Fever reducer, Antimalarial properties. This plant has been used by Filipino communities for generations and continues to be an important part of traditional healing practices.

waray.description: An Mahogany in usa nga dako nga tropical tree nga may compound nga mga bulong ngan valuable hardwood. An bark ngan mga seeds in mayda traditional medicinal uses.
preparation_steps: Kalad-an an bark para ha tsaa
waray.cultural_relevance: An Mahogany in ginkikilala para ha iya timber ngan haluag nga gintatanim bug-os han tropics.
waray.detailed_description: An Mahogany (Swietenia macrophylla) in usa nga trees plant nga gingagamit ha traditional nga paagi ha Philippine traditional nga tambal. Ginkilala ini para ha iya medicinal nga mga kabtang upod an Treats diarrhea, Fever reducer, Antimalarial properties. Ini nga tanom in gingagamit han Filipino communities para ha mga henerasyon ngan nagpapabilin nga importante nga parte han traditional healing practices.

Narra.name: Narra
Narra.description: Narra is the national tree of the Philippines. It has distinctive disc-shaped seed pods and produces red sap (kino).
Narra.cultural_relevance: Narra is the Philippines' national tree, known for its beautiful timber and medicinal red sap.
Narra.detailed_description: Narra (Pterocarpus indicus) is a trees plant traditionally used in Philippine traditional medicine. It is known for its medicinal properties including Treats mouth sores, Sore throat remedy, Antiseptic wash. This plant has been used by Filipino communities for generations and continues to be an important part of traditional healing practices.

waray.description: An Narra in an national tree han Pilipinas. Mayda ini distinctive disc-shaped nga seed pods ngan nagbubuhat hin red sap (kino).
preparation_steps: I-apply an sap ha mga wound
waray.cultural_relevance: An Narra in an national tree han Pilipinas, ginkilala para ha iya beautiful timber ngan medicinal red sap.
waray.detailed_description: An Narra (Pterocarpus indicus) in usa nga trees plant nga gingagamit ha traditional nga paagi ha Philippine traditional nga tambal. Ginkilala ini para ha iya medicinal nga mga kabtang upod an Treats mouth sores, Sore throat remedy, Antiseptic wash. Ini nga tanom in gingagamit han Filipino communities para ha mga henerasyon ngan nagpapabilin nga importante nga parte han traditional healing practices.

Rosal.name: Rosal
